opp.name: Aegon_S2P
opp.ais_why:
• Apr 2026 Avoma call captures Aegon's Eric Verschut explicitly stating: 'strong interest exists in AI adoption ‚Äî particularly guided intake and agentic workflows' with human-control framing. Eric also asked sophisticated technical questions on AI semantic layers and integration. This is a verbatim buyer-side AI-adoption statement plus deep AI-architecture inquiry ‚Äî a textbook AI-Hungry buyer signal.
• AI-rich product list (ANA, Agentic AI, Merlin, Merlin Intake plus iSave/iRequest/eInvoicing/eProcurement and the full S2P suite) at $250K. BD_Engagement is Engaged. LastActivityDate is 2026-05-06. The customer has accepted a generative-AI roadshow invitation plus a demo/POC follow-up is scheduled ‚Äî multiple AI-themed touchpoints in the calendar.
• High confidence: the call documents AI as an explicit selection theme with a named buyer-side champion (Eric Verschut) using AI-architecture vocabulary (semantic layers). Held at 8 (not 9) because the evaluation is still open between suite-vs-best-of-breed and there is no formal RFP or POC commitment yet. A signed POC scope or RFP-with-AI-mandate would push to 9.

sources: Eric Verschut, 29 Apr 2026 call (pain point: SAP wait times vs agility need)
Eric Verschut, 29 Apr 2026 call (org size: 12k employees, 100k agents, 45-50 procurement team)
Avoma notes 29 Apr 2026
metrics.narrative: Eric Verschut articulated operational pain around slow SAP feature delivery and the need for faster updates and client feedback incorporation into the roadmap (29 Apr 2026 call: 'lengthy wait times experienced with SAP for new features' vs 'current system allows for quicker updates'). No quantified savings, cycle-time reduction, headcount impact, or ROI stated. Aegon is a 12,000-employee company with 100,000 external sales agents and a 45-50 person global procurement team (29 Apr call); Eric manages 20-25 people across Netherlands, Hungary, UK, US. The business problem centers on agility, integration complexity across a multi-system landscape (SAP Ariba, Oracle Cloud, legacy), and readiness for AI-led intake/agentic workflows. Quantifying the value case (cost of delay from SAP friction, potential savings from intake automation, integration cost avoidance) is a critical gap and must become a ranked move.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Eric Verschut, Decision Maker
Eric Verschut, 29 Apr 2026 call (deep engagement, pain articulation, AI interest, demo request)
Avoma notes 29 Apr 2026 (action items: demo, roadshow invitation)
Evidence gap: no advocacy evidence, no multi-thread, 39-day silence post-18 May
champion.narrative: Eric Verschut (Head of Procurement Operations, Decision Maker contact role) is the single engaged contact. On 29 Apr 2026 he led a detailed discovery call, shared org structure (20-25 person team, 12k employees, 100k agents), articulated pain (SAP delays, integration complexity), confirmed strong AI interest ('strong interest... provided clear use cases and human control points'), and requested follow-up (demo, AI roadshow invitation). He has operational authority and deep requirements knowledge, positioning him as a potential champion. However, champion strength is 'developing' because: (1) no evidence yet of Eric advocating for Zycus internally, introducing us to the EB/CTO stakeholders, or pushing back against Oracle/Ariba; (2) no multi-threading beyond Eric (single contact role, no other buyer attendees on the 29 Apr call); (3) 39 days of silence since 18 May last activity with no follow-up on the promised demo/roadshow, raising cooling risk. Building Eric into an active champion requires deepening the relationship (quantified value case, exec intro facilitation, competitive wedge coaching) and securing his introduction to the relevant stakeholder (SVP IT & Procurement) or Pascal Koppenhagen (CTO Corporate Center) to multi-thread and map the EB.

sources: Salesforce Competitors__c: Oracle, SAP Ariba
Eric Verschut, 29 Apr 2026 call (SAP Ariba/Oracle Cloud integration discussion, SAP pain point)
Avoma notes 29 Apr 2026 (SAP feature-delivery friction, multi-system landscape)
competition.narrative: Competing against Oracle and SAP Ariba per Salesforce Competitors__c field, corroborated by the 29 Apr call context (Aegon's current landscape includes SAP Ariba and Oracle Cloud, and Eric discussed integration with both). SAP Ariba is the primary threat: it is embedded in Aegon's stack, Eric cited SAP's slow feature delivery as a pain point, and Aegon is evaluating whether to consolidate on a single suite (which could favor the incumbent) or move to best-of-breed (which opens the door for Zycus). Oracle is present as a peer alternative. No other competitors named. The wedge: Zycus's agility (faster updates, client-driven roadmap vs SAP's rigidity), AI-native design (Merlin/agentic intake vs Oracle/Ariba's bolt-on AI), and API-first integration to avoid master-data lock-in. The activation: position the 29 Apr roadshow and demo as a head-to-head proof that Zycus delivers AI-led intake and cross-system orchestration faster and with less complexity than SAP/Oracle can retrofit. However, with 39 days of silence and no recent competitive positioning, the wedge has not been driven home; refreshing it is urgent.

sources: Eric Verschut, 29 Apr 2026 call (SAP wait times vs agility need, quote: 'lengthy wait times... contrasting sharply with... quicker updates')
Eric Verschut, 29 Apr 2026 call (integration pain: 'seamless integration via APIs... without duplicating master data')
Eric Verschut, 29 Apr 2026 call (architecture uncertainty: suite vs best-of-breed, vendor master location)
identify_pain.narrative: Eric Verschut (Head of Procurement Operations, pain owner) articulated two operational pains on 29 Apr 2026. First, slow feature delivery from SAP: 'lengthy wait times experienced with SAP for new features' vs the need for 'quicker updates and incorporation of client feedback into the development roadmap'. Second, integration complexity in a multi-system landscape (SAP Ariba, Oracle Cloud, legacy tools) without master-data duplication, creating friction and manual reconciliation. A third, strategic pain is architectural uncertainty: Aegon is evaluating suite vs best-of-breed and where to host the vendor master table (Zycus, Oracle, or an external data lake), which delays vendor selection and RFP launch. These pains are confirmed from the buyer's own words; quantifying their operational and financial cost (cycle time, manual effort, vendor onboarding delays) would strengthen the value case.

sources: Evidence gap: no MSA, SOW, legal, InfoSec, or contracting discussion on 29 Apr call or in SF fields
Eric Verschut, 29 Apr 2026 call (iRequest contract ends 'next year', no exact date)
paper_process.narrative: No paper process detail emerged on the 29 Apr call or in Salesforce. MSA/SOW mechanics, legal review steps, InfoSec questionnaire, procurement channel (direct vs through an SI/partner), and contracting timeline are all unknown. Aegon's procurement operations team (20-25 people across Netherlands, Hungary, UK, US, per 29 Apr call) likely runs a structured contracting process for $250k deals, but it has not been discussed. The iRequest contract end date ('next year' as of Apr 2026, likely 2027) could serve as a renewal/replacement forcing function, but the exact date and its impact on paper-process urgency are unconfirmed. Clarifying contracting mechanics and lead times should follow EB identification and commercial discussions.

sources: OpportunityContactRole (1 contact: Eric Verschut, Decision Maker; no EB/Executive Sponsor)
Avoma 29 Apr 2026 call (no EB discussion, no commercial/budget conversation)
Account contacts list (Fred Cazemier, Pascal Koppenhagen, Nicole Grootveld-Sandig present but not opp-linked)
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified across Contact Roles, Avoma, or Salesforce fields. Eric Verschut (Head of Procurement Operations, Decision Maker role) engaged on operational and technical requirements but did not discuss budget, pricing, or commercial approval authority on the 29 Apr call. Account-level contacts include Fred Cazemier (SVP IT and Procurement), Pascal Koppenhagen (CTO Corporate Center), and Nicole Grootveld-Sandig (Global CTO), but none are contact-role-linked to this opportunity and none appeared on the 29 Apr call. The 29 Apr call focused on landscape design and technical fit; no EB/CFO/sponsor emerged. For a $250k, 15-month horizon deal, identifying and engaging the budget authority is essential and must be a top-3 move.

sources: Eric Verschut, 29 Apr 2026 call (contract end, landscape design 2024, RFP Q1 2025 plan)
Avoma notes 29 Apr 2026 (design underway, RFP/ROI planned Q1 2024 )
Evidence gap: no updated timeline, no approval-chain data, no subsequent process step visible
decision_process.narrative: Eric Verschut stated on 29 Apr 2026 that Aegon's iRequest contract ends 'next year' (interpreted as 2027 given the call date), the future procurement landscape was being designed in 2024, and an RFP was planned for Q1 2025. That timeline is now 16+ months stale with no update. The 29 Apr call was a single discovery session; no subsequent validation, shortlist, or EB review has occurred. The approval chain is unknown (no data on multi-level sign-off, procurement vs IT vs finance gates, or board involvement for a $250k initiative). The process appears gated on completing the architecture design (suite vs best-of-breed, integration model) before formal vendor evaluation can begin. Refreshing the timeline, mapping the approval path, and securing the next milestone (demo, RFP kickoff, or BRD review) are immediate needs.

sources: Eric Verschut, 29 Apr 2026 call (integration, architecture, vendor master, AI requirements)
Avoma notes 29 Apr 2026 (sustainability, AI control points, agentic workflows)
Avoma notes 29 Apr 2026 (suite vs best-of-breed, future landscape design underway)
decision_criteria.narrative: Eric Verschut outlined several evaluation priorities on 29 Apr 2026: seamless API integration to connect Zycus with SAP Ariba, Oracle Cloud, and legacy systems without master-data duplication; a single suite vs best-of-breed architecture decision pending; vendor master table location (Zycus-owned vs Oracle vs external data lake); and AI integration with a context layer to interpret business language and maintain human control points. Interest in guided intake, agentic workflows, and data-quality improvements was confirmed ('strong interest... provided clear use cases and human control points are maintained', 29 Apr call). Sustainability (carbon emissions tracking retained for investor relations, broader efforts scaled back per US governance changes, 29 Apr call) is a secondary criterion. No formal scorecard, RFP rubric, or weighted criteria shared; the stated design-in-progress from 2024 and Q1 2025 RFP timeline (now 16+ months old) suggests criteria are still evolving. Securing the updated, written BRD/RFP criteria is a near-term priority.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

items.action: Deliver the overdue AI roadshow invitation and demo to Eric Verschut within 3 business days
items.trigger: Eric Verschut requested the generative AI roadshow invitation and demo on 29 Apr 2026; commitments overdue by 58 days. LastActivityDate 18 May (39 days ago). Deal cooling with single contact and no buyer re-engagement.
items.expected_effect: Re-ignite momentum with the Decision Maker, demonstrate Zycus is responsive and still engaged, and prove the AI/agility wedge vs SAP/Oracle. Failure to deliver now risks Eric advancing competing vendors or deprioritizing Zycus entirely.

items.action: Quantify the agility and AI value case with Eric Verschut: model cost of SAP delay, integration savings, and Merlin intake ROI
items.trigger: Eric stated pain (SAP feature-delivery delays, integration complexity) on 29 Apr 2026 but no quantified savings, cycle-time reduction, or ROI. No metrics identified in MEDDPICC.
items.expected_effect: Build the business case to advance the deal past discovery. Quantified ROI is essential to secure EB engagement and justify $250k investment. Ties the pain Eric articulated to a defendable financial outcome, positioning Zycus as the measurable alternative to SAP/Oracle.

items.action: Secure Eric Verschut's introduction to the SVP IT and Procurement or CTO to validate the AI and integration vision at the exec level

Fred Cazemier (SVP IT & Procurement), Pascal Koppenhagen (CTO Corporate Center), Nicole Grootveld-Sandig (Global CTO) are account contacts but not opp-linked. No exec engagement in 58 days.
items.expected_effect: Multi-thread to the Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor, map budget authority, and de-risk the single-contact dependency. A $250k, full-suite deal 15 months out requires exec alignment; staying single-threaded with an operational lead is a disqualify risk. Exec validation of the AI/integration story is the gate to moving past discovery.

items.action: Refresh the RFP and contract-end timeline with Eric Verschut and confirm where Aegon landed on suite vs best-of-breed architecture
items.trigger: Eric stated on 29 Apr 2026 that contract ends 'next year', landscape designed in 2024, RFP planned Q1 2025. Timeline now 16+ months stale. Aegon evaluating suite vs best-of-breed and vendor master location (Zycus, Oracle, external lake), which gates vendor selection.
items.expected_effect: Clarify true urgency and decision timeline. Without an updated RFP plan and architecture decision, the Sep 2027 close date is a placeholder, not a credible milestone. Understanding where Aegon landed (suite vs best-of-breed, vendor master location) allows Zycus to position the API-first, AI-native solution as the right fit for either path and to forecast the deal realistically.

items.action: Position the Merlin agentic intake and API-first integration wedge as the modern alternative to SAP Ariba and Oracle retrofit AI
items.trigger: Competing against SAP Ariba (incumbent, high threat, slow feature delivery pain) and Oracle (peer alternative). No competitive positioning in 39 days since 18 May. Eric confirmed AI appetite (guided intake, agentic workflows, context layer) on 29 Apr call.
items.expected_effect: Differentiate Zycus on the two dimensions Eric cares about: agility (faster roadmap, client-driven updates vs SAP rigidity) and AI-native design (Merlin agentic workflows, context-layer orchestration across SAP/Oracle/legacy vs competitors' bolt-on AI). Make the demo a head-to-head proof that Zycus delivers what SAP/Oracle cannot retrofit. Without this wedge activation, Aegon defaults to the incumbent or Oracle.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 29 Apr 2026, qualified same day (58 days in Qualified stage), close 30 Sep 2027 (461 days out). Last verified buyer touch 18 May 2026 (39 days ago). Single discovery call 29 Apr, then silence. No EB mapped, no champion secured, architecture still being designed in Q1 2025 cadence with RFP planned for Q1 2025 per 29 Apr call (now 16+ months stale). Pace required: advance to Formal Evaluation within 90 days, secure EB/champion by Q3 2026 to stay credible for late-2027 close. Current pace: zero movement in 39 days.
evidence: Single buyer touchpoint 29 Apr 2026, no follow-up in 39 days (source: LastActivityDate 18 May, Avoma call 29 Apr)
Buyer stated contract ends 'next year' and RFP planned Q1 2025 on 29 Apr 2026 call, timeline now 16+ months stale with no refresh (source: Eric Verschut, 29 Apr call)
Decision Maker Eric Verschut engaged once, no Economic Buyer identified, single-threaded (source: OpportunityContactRole)
Critical architecture decisions unresolved: suite vs best-of-breed, vendor master data location (Zycus vs Oracle vs external lake), integration strategy undefined (source: Eric Verschut, 29 Apr call)
AI appetite confirmed but gated on use-case clarity and human control (source: Eric Verschut, 29 Apr call)
Competing against Oracle and SAP Ariba in incumbent/peer landscape; no recent competitive positioning (source: SF Competitors__c, 29 Apr call context)
north_star_verdict.headline: Qualified deal 15 months from close remains single-threaded with no buyer contact in 39 days after lone discovery call. Architecture decisions unresolved, no champion or EB secured, SAP Ariba incumbent advantage grows without competitive repositioning or multi-threading.

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage deal with strong AI fit but stalled for 39 days. Critical gaps: no EB, single-threaded, no champion advocacy evidence, stale timeline, no quantified ROI. Highest-impact levers: re-engage Eric with the overdue demo/roadshow and a quantified agility/AI value case, secure his intro to the SVP IT & Procurement or CTO to multi-thread and map the EB, and refresh the RFP/architecture timeline to understand true urgency.

competitive_position.summary: Competing against SAP Ariba (primary threat, incumbent with integration and slow-feature-delivery pain) and Oracle (peer alternative). SAP Ariba is the highest current threat: deeply embedded in Aegon's landscape, Eric cited its rigidity as a pain point on 29 Apr 2026, and Aegon's suite-vs-best-of-breed evaluation could favor the incumbent if Zycus does not prove a compelling alternative. Oracle is a medium threat as a known peer option. No evidence of GEP, Coupa, or other procurement platforms. Last competitive discussion 29 Apr (58 days ago); no recent positioning, no wedge activation in 39 days. Zycus must re-engage with the AI/agility wedge (Merlin agentic intake, API-first integration, faster roadmap vs SAP/Oracle retrofit) to stay viable.

deltas.subject: Re-engage the cooling buyer: 39 days since last activity (18 May 2026), no follow-up on promised demo/roadshow. Risk: Eric Verschut (single contact, Decision Maker) loses interest or advances competing vendors while Zycus is silent. Lever: deliver the overdue AI roadshow invitation and demo within 7 days, paired with a quantified value case (SAP delay cost, integration savings, AI ROI) to reignite momentum and prove Zycus is the agile, AI-native alternative to SAP/Oracle.

Refresh the timeline and close the architecture-decision loop: Eric stated on 29 Apr 2026 that the contract ends 'next year', the landscape was designed in 2024, and the RFP was planned for Q1 2025. That timeline is now 16+ months stale. Aegon is evaluating suite vs best-of-breed and vendor master location (Zycus, Oracle, external lake), which gates vendor selection. Lever: re-open the conversation with Eric to confirm the current RFP/contract timeline, understand where Aegon landed on architecture (suite vs best-of-breed), and position Zycus as the API-first, AI-native solution that fits either path. Without timeline clarity the close date (Sep 2027) is a placeholder, not a plan.

deltas.to: Re-engage the cooling buyer: 39 days since last activity (18 May 2026), no follow-up on promised demo/roadshow. Risk: Eric Verschut (single contact, Decision Ma
deltas.subject: Re-engage the cooling buyer: 39 days since last activity (18 May 2026), no follow-up on promised demo/roadshow. Risk: Eric Verschut (single contact, Decision Maker) loses interest or advances competing vendors while Zycus is silent. Lever: deliver the overdue AI roadshow invitation and demo within 7 days, paired with a quantified value case (SAP delay cost, integration savings, AI ROI) to reignite momentum and prove Zycus is the agile, AI-native alternative to SAP/Oracle.

deltas.to: Multi-thread to the Economic Buyer and CTO: single contact role (Eric Verschut, Head of Procurement Operations) with no EB identified and no access to the relev
deltas.subject: Multi-thread to the Economic Buyer and CTO: single contact role (Eric Verschut, Head of Procurement Operations) with no EB identified and no access to the relevant stakeholder (SVP IT & Procurement), Pascal Koppenhagen (CTO Corporate Center), or Nicole Grootveld-Sandig (Global CTO). Lever: ask Eric to introduce the CTO or SVP to validate the AI/integration vision at the exec level and map budget authority. A $250k, full-suite deal 15 months out requires exec sponsorship; staying single-threaded with an operational lead is a disqualify risk.

deltas.to: Refresh the timeline and close the architecture-decision loop: Eric stated on 29 Apr 2026 that the contract ends 'next year', the landscape was designed in 2024
deltas.subject: Refresh the timeline and close the architecture-decision loop: Eric stated on 29 Apr 2026 that the contract ends 'next year', the landscape was designed in 2024, and the RFP was planned for Q1 2025. That timeline is now 16+ months stale. Aegon is evaluating suite vs best-of-breed and vendor master location (Zycus, Oracle, external lake), which gates vendor selection. Lever: re-open the conversation with Eric to confirm the current RFP/contract timeline, understand where Aegon landed on architecture (suite vs best-of-breed), and position Zycus as the API-first, AI-native solution that fits either path. Without timeline clarity the close date (Sep 2027) is a placeholder, not a plan.

value.name: SAP Ariba
value.quote: Lengthy wait times experienced with SAP for new features, contrasting sharply with the need for quicker updates and incorporation of client feedback into the development roadmap
value.source: Eric Verschut, 29 Apr 2026 call; Salesforce Competitors__c
value.how_we_win: Agility wedge: Zycus delivers faster feature updates and client-driven roadmap vs SAP rigidity. AI activation: position Merlin agentic intake and API-first integration as the modern alternative to SAP's slow, monolithic stack. Make the demo/roadshow a head-to-head proof of speed and AI-native design.

packets.subject: SAP Ariba

value.name: Oracle
value.quote: Seamless integration via APIs is critical to connect Zycus with SAP Ariba, Oracle Cloud and legacy systems without duplicating master data
value.source: Eric Verschut, 29 Apr 2026 call; Salesforce Competitors__c
value.how_we_win: Integration wedge: Zycus's API-first, no-master-data-lock-in approach vs Oracle's proprietary stack. AI differentiation: Merlin's agentic workflows and context layer for cross-system orchestration, which Oracle's AI cannot match at Aegon's required depth.

packets.subject: Oracle

scope

gaps: No Economic Buyer identified across Contact Roles, Avoma, or Salesforce fields
Only 1 buyer-side contact role on opp (Eric Verschut); 21 account contacts not opp-linked
No quantified value case (savings, cycle-time reduction, ROI) despite confirmed pain
No paper-process detail (MSA, SOW, legal, InfoSec, contracting lead times)
Timeline stated on 29 Apr 2026 (contract end 'next year', RFP Q1 2025) is 16+ months stale with no update
Architecture decision (suite vs best-of-breed, vendor master location) unresolved, gates vendor selection
validation gate: a named person in a to-do/action item is in no Salesforce contact role, Avoma attendee list, or active-user roster and is anchored to no source (offending: 'Fred Cazemier')
evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: 365d field history, 90d tasks/events, all contact roles
